title: Dismembered human remains found in Shirley identified as 69-year-old man
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Dismembered remains in Shirley identified as 69-year-old Peter Degan, according to Middlesex DA Marian Ryan.
Degan, recently living in Rockland, had previous cocaine trafficking charges; foul play is suspected in his death.
Shirley police and State Police are investigating; public urged to report sightings of Degan or suspicious activity.

title: ‘State-sanctioned cruelty’: Chelsea family sues ICE after Mother’s Day arrest last year
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A Chelsea family is suing ICE over a Mother's Day arrest involving excessive force and window smashing.
The lawsuit follows a required administrative complaint with no response after six months, enabling federal court action.
The incident left the family traumatized, with children experiencing anxiety and nightmares, affecting their daily lives.

title: In rare sightings, scientists spot blue whales in waters off Martha’s Vineyard
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Scientists from the New England Aquarium spotted blue whales off southern New England, marking a rare occurrence for the region.
Aerial surveys recorded blue whale sightings near the Northeast Canyons Marine National Monument and 15 miles south of Martha's Vineyard.
Blue whale sightings outside of their typical Canadian feeding grounds are uncommon, highlighting changing oceanographic conditions in the area.

title: One person found, one still missing after fishing boat overturns off Cape Cod
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Coast Guard retrieved one unconscious person after the Yankee Rose overturned near Race Point, Provincetown.
Search efforts continue for a second person missing after the fishing boat capsized off Cape Cod's coast.
The Yankee Rose recently faced another incident involving carbon monoxide poisoning, leading to hospitalizations last week.

title: Fenway location of Regina Pizzeria has closed
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Regina Pizzeria's Fenway location at 1330 Boylston St. has closed after ownership chose not to renew the lease.
The Fenway site opened in 2013 as part of Regina's expansion, becoming one of 20 locations in Massachusetts.
Regina Pizzeria now has 12 locations in Massachusetts, primarily quick-service, maintaining its historic North End presence.

title: Mansfield man killed in crash on I-95 in Attleboro, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chad Askew, 43, from Mansfield, died after his Honda Civic hit a tractor-trailer on I-95 North early Wednesday.
The crash occurred near Exit 4 in Attleboro; no other motorists required medical attention, officials reported.
Massachusetts State Police are seeking a man who fled the scene wearing shorts and boots; investigation continues.

title: Newly released video shows crash that left pickup truck dangling over Zakim Bridge
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts Department of Transportation released video of a Feb. 5 crash on Zakim Bridge involving a pickup truck.
The truck swerved to avoid a disabled vehicle, stopping at the bridge edge due to a snowbank and fence.
A heavy-duty tow truck secured the pickup on the bridge; no injuries were reported in the incident.

title: A Mass. college is buying a local golf course. Here’s why.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Stonehill College is buying the 150-acre Easton Country Club to support academics, athletics, and community engagement.
The golf course will remain semi-private, open to Stonehill affiliates and the local golfing community, with existing operations retained.
The acquisition will support women’s and men’s golf programs and create learning opportunities for Stonehill students.

title: A deported dad, a city in chaos, and a Tinder sting
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Christina Toledo and Nexan Aroldo Asencio Corado face family challenges after Nexan's deportation to Guatemala.
Reporter Chris Muther recounts being in Puerto Vallarta during violence sparked by the killing of a drug cartel leader.
Patricia Wen investigates college students' use of Tinder for a sting operation with unintended consequences.

title: Boston restaurant hit with brief license suspension after hosting Patriots players’ raucous party
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Estella Restaurant in Boston faced a one-day liquor license suspension after hosting a party with Patriots players and strippers.
Violations included indoor smoking, unauthorized adult entertainment, and after-hours liquor consumption; two days of suspension are held in abeyance.
Owner Helder George Brandao held responsible, but claims players brought their own drinks and left without paying tabs.
